title: pellaea geraniifolia
intro: Pellaea geraniifolia, also known as the fern-leaved pebble, is a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. It is native to Australia and is often found in rocky areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to help with wound healing and skin conditions. Pellaea geraniifolia can be used in herbal preparations such as teas and tinctures, and is believed to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving overall well-being.
